PreToolUse.pattern: rm -rf|git reset --hard|git push --force|git checkout \.|git clean -f|drop table|truncate
PreToolUse.message: Destructive operation blocked. Use /careful mode or confirm explicitly.
Skipping hooks/signing is blocked. Fix the underlying issue instead.

PreToolUse.description: Command injection detection — block suspicious bash patterns
PreToolUse.message: Potential command injection detected. Bash command contains suspicious patterns (subshell + network, eval, chained backticks). Review and simplify the command.

PreToolUse.description: Block dev server starts (agents should never start long-running processes)
PreToolUse.pattern: runserver|npm run dev|yarn dev|bun run dev|flask run|uvicorn.*--reload
PreToolUse.message: Agents must not start dev servers. Dev servers are started by the user, not the agent.

PreToolUse.description: Detect hardcoded secrets before they enter code
PreToolUse.message: Secret detected in code. Use environment variables (os.environ) instead.

PreToolUse.description: TDD guard — warn if writing code without test first
PreToolUse.message: TDD: Writing implementation code. Verify the TEST was written FIRST. If not, write the test before this code.

PreToolUse.description: Block writes to config files without confirmation
PreToolUse.message: Modifying config file. Verify this is intentional.
File exceeds 300-line limit. Consider splitting into smaller modules.

PostToolUse.message: File modified — checkpoint available for rollback

PostToolUse.description: Remind sync check after code changes
Check for zero-debt violations after write
PostToolUse.message: Technical debt marker found. Implement now or create a GitHub Issue.
If this change revealed a non-obvious pattern or gotcha, capture it now with /learn.
Commit message must follow conventional format: type(scope): description (feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore)

PostToolUse.description: Drift prevention — reinforce current task context after every Bash/Read
PostToolUse.message: <system-reminder>Current task: check your [REQ-xxx]. Phase: check SDLC stage. Rules: TDD first, handoff format, /learn insights. NEVER code from training data — verify with context7.</system-reminder>

PostToolUse.description: TDD drift check — warn if code written without test
PostToolUse.message: <system-reminder>TDD CHECK: Did you write the TEST first? If not, write the test NOW before continuing with implementation code.</system-reminder>

SessionStart.description: Load playbook, rules, and ethos at session start
Load last checkpoint and project index if available

SessionStart.message: Session started. Loaded: playbook, rules, ethos. Run /sc:load for full context restoration.

SessionStart.description: Context front-loading — summarize current state before any work
SessionStart.command: Summarize current project state in under 50 characters. Detect: is this a new topic or continuation of previous work? Load appropriate context.
SessionStart.message: <system-reminder>CONTEXT FRONT-LOAD: Before doing ANY work, orient yourself. What project? What phase? What was last done? What's next? Read CLAUDE.md and last checkpoint.</system-reminder>

Stop.message: Session ending. Run /learn for new insights. Run /sc:save to persist session context.

PreCompact.message: Context compacting — verify: current task, spec section, test status, and any in-progress work are preserved.

UserPromptSubmit.description: Remind about handoff format after implementation
UserPromptSubmit.message: Implementation done? Use the standardized handoff format (docs/patterns/handoff-protocol.md).
